title: RplOption.to_bytes() doesn't validate type range
description:
File: python/src/lichen/rpl/messages.py:73-80

The RplOption.to_bytes() method doesn't validate that the option type fits in a single byte. If type > 255, the bytes() call will raise an OverflowError at runtime.

Current code:
```python
def to_bytes(self) -> bytes:
    if self.type == RplOptionType.PAD1:
        if self.data:
            raise RplError("Pad1 option carries no data")
        return b"\x00"
    if len(self.data) > 0xFF:
        raise RplError(f"option data too long: {len(self.data)} bytes")
    return bytes([self.type, len(self.data)]) + self.data  # type not validated
```

While RplOptionType enum values are all < 256, the type field accepts any int. An RplOption created with type=256 would fail with an unclear error from bytes().

Fix: Add explicit validation:
```python
if not 0 <= self.type <= 255:
    raise RplError(f"option type out of range: {self.type}")
```
close_reason: Fixed: Added type range validation (0-255) in RplOption.to_bytes() before bytes() call
